The TCB-RCA baseline centers on frontend due to no timing evidence, so it does not override the structural plausibility of checkoutservice as the root cause.

metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends directly on multiple downstream services. A fault in any of these dependencies can propagate to frontend, causing the symptom without requiring a fault in an upstream service. The topology shows no explicit downstream of frontend beyond its dependencies, so plausible root causes are any of its dependent services. Blast radius remains confined to frontend in this topology, while monitoring should prioritize frontend and its direct dependencies as the most probable propagation paths.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources are weakly aligned on a plausible propagation path but disagree on the actual root cause. The topology data supports checkoutservice as a plausible upstream cause of the frontend symptom, while the Log Agent presents no observable errors and the TCB-RCA baseline centers on frontend with no timing evidence. Given the unobserved root cause concept and structural plausibility, checkoutservice is favored as the root cause candidate, with low overall confidence due to lack of corroborating logs and timing signals. The propagation path from checkoutservice to frontend is consistent with the topology constraints and the observed symptom.
